description_long: This object is a classic percussion cap revolver, featuring a long, blued steel barrel and a six-chambered cylinder. The frame and trigger guard are made of brass or a brass-like alloy, providing a distinct contrast to the dark barrel and cylinder. The grip is crafted from polished wood, shaped ergonomically to fit comfortably in the hand, and has a rich brown finish. The hammer is exposed and sits above the grip, ready to be cocked for firing. The trigger guard is rounded and made of brass, encircling a simple, curved trigger. The overall design is reminiscent of 19th-century Colt revolvers, with a robust and elegant construction suitable for historical or period urban settings.
description: A classic percussion cap revolver with a long blued barrel, brass frame, and polished wooden grip. The design is robust and elegant, featuring an exposed hammer and rounded brass trigger guard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the barrel and the alignment of the cylinder.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the barrel, the six-chambered cylinder, brass frame, and wooden grip.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back of the wooden grip, the hammer, and the top of the frame.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the barrel, cylinder, brass frame, and wooden grip from the other side.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long and round with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized brass guard with a smooth contour and no finger grooves
